title: carex longii var. meridionalis
intro: Carex longii var. meridionalis, also known as Northern Sedge, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The Northern Sedge has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing swelling and promoting wound healing, as well as having potential antimicrobial and antifungal effects. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional medicine, often in combination with other herbs.
